prompt: Run the suite: TEST_PROJECT=healer-test npx playwright test homepage.spec.ts --reporter=list. Read projects/healer-test/test-results.json if present. For each failure decide: is it a TEST-CASE issue (e.g. wrong selector/timing) or a real APPLICATION defect? Write specs/test-analysis.md with the classification. Only log genuine APP defects to docs/issues.csv. If it is a test-side issue, hand off to the Test Healer. Report the verdict.
output:
I'll start by reading the project configuration files and then execute the tests. Now let me run the test suite. The root `playwright.config.ts` is being used but without a `--config` flag pointing to the root. The tests are run from within the project dir so `baseURL` (`TEST_URL=https://example.com`) isn't being picked up — it's loading from the project `.env` via root config only when run from the workspace root. Clear results. Now I have all the information I need. **Analysis:** - **TC-001** ✅ PASSED — correct `h1` selector, text matches "Example Domain" - **TC-002** ❌ FAILED — `#main-title` selector finds nothing; `project-memory.md` already confirms the heading is a plain `

` with no id. This is a **wrong-selector / test-case issue**, NOT an app defect. No genuine app defect → `docs/issues.csv` gets no new rows. Writing `specs/test-analysis.md` and handing off to the Healer. The JSON reporter did not write a file (the output path from the root config is `path.join(projectRoot, 'test-results.json')` — i.e. `projects/healer-test/test-results.json` — but the file wasn't flushed; that's fine, we have all the data from the list output). Now I have enough to write the analysis. **Phase 2 — Analysis results:** | Test | Status | Duration | Error | |---|---|---|---| | TC-001: homepage heading text | ✅ PASS | 319ms | — | | TC-002: heading is present | ❌ FAIL | 5.2s | `locator('#main-title')` — element not found | **Root cause for TC-002:** The locator `#main-title` does not exist on `example.com`. The page heading is a plain `

` with no `id` attribute. This is a **wrong selector** — a **TEST-CASE issue**, not an application defect. The application correctly renders `

Example Domain

`; only the test selector is wrong. The fix is to replace `#main-title` with `h1` (confirmed working in TC-001). Now I'll write `specs/test-analysis.md` and hand off to the Healer. --- ## ✅ Phase 1 — Execution Complete Suite ran successfully from workspace root: ``` TEST_PROJECT=healer-test npx playwright test homepage.spec.ts --reporter=list ``` | # | Test | Status | Duration | |---|---|---|---| | TC-001 | homepage heading text | ✅ **PASS** | 319 ms | | TC-002 | heading is present | ❌ **FAIL** | 5.2 s | --- ## 🔍 Phase 2 — Analysis **TC-002** failed with: ``` Locator: locator('#main-title') Error: element(s) not found — timeout 5000ms ``` **Root-cause category:** `Selector` **Classification: TEST-CASE ISSUE** — the application is working correctly. Evidence: - TC-001 navigates to the **exact same URL**, uses `h1`, and **passes** with `"Example Domain"`. - `https://example.com` has a plain `

Example Domain

` — no `id="main-title"` exists anywhere on the page. - `project-memory.md` already documents this as a known wrong selector in TC-002. This is **not** an application defect → **not** logged to `docs/issues.csv`. --- ## 🔀 Phase 3 — Branch: HAND-OFF TO TEST HEALER `specs/test-analysis.md` has been written with the full failure table, root-cause, and the precise fix. ### Required fix for the Healer | Item | Detail | |---|---| | **File** | `projects/healer-test/tests/homepage.spec.ts` | | **Line** | 14 | | **Current selector** | `#main-title` | | **Fix** | Replace with `h1` | | **Confidence** | High — `h1` confirmed working in TC-001 on same page | | **App defect?** | ❌ No — `docs/issues.csv` unchanged | ➡️ **Handing off to `playwright-test-healer`** to apply the selector fix. After healing, the Executor & Analyser must re-run the suite to close the loop.
